meaning: n.使确实,确证的事实,确证
examples: He could not get a single witness to establish independent corroboration of his version of the accident.
The corroboration appears online in the journal Physical Review Letters.
The witness was unable to provide corroboration f what he had told the police.
The second victim could not be traced in order to provide up to date corroboration.
So far as I can tell there is no corroboration from any other student or parent.
